title: senecio pinnatus var. leptolobus
intro: Senecio pinnatus var. leptolobus, also known as the fern-leaf Jacob's ladder or fern-leaf ragwort, is a perennial plant native to the western United States and Canada. It belongs to the daisy family and is characterized by its fern-like leaves and tall spikes of yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, which is said to provide health benefits like relieving coughs, reducing fever, and promoting digestion.
